mappings.rationale: Covers the literary developments across the T'ang, Sung, Yuan, Ming, and Ch'ing periods discussed in subsections A1b, A3, B2a, C1c, and C2b.
Directly relevant to subsection A3 on the resurgence of Neo-Confucianism during the Sung dynasty and its continued importance under Ming and Ch'ing.
Directly covers subsection B on Mongol-Chinese rule under the Yuan dynasty including Genghis Khan's conquest and Kublai Khan's establishment of the dynasty.
Relevant to subsections A1b and B2a on the growing influence of Buddhism during the late T'ang and Yuan periods.
Provides context for understanding the Ming and Ch'ing dynasties' legacy and the tributary system discussed in subsection C.
Relevant to subsections B1b and B2b on the expansion of trade and commercial contacts with the West during Yuan China.
Relevant to the dynastic patterns of the Five Dynasties, Sung, Yuan, Ming, and Ch'ing that structure this section.
Relevant to subsection C2a on early Ch'ing foreign relations and contacts with the West.
Relevant to subsections A1c and C2b on Chinese economic developments including agricultural advances, trade expansion, and manufacturing.
